2605.14766.title: Streaming Speech-to-Text Translation with a SpeechLLM
2605.14766.paper_summary: Normally, a system that translates speech into text consists of separate modules for speech recognition and text-to-text translation. Combining those tasks into a SpeechLLM promises to exploit paralinguistic information in the speech and to reduce cascaded errors. But existing SpeechLLM systems are slow since they do not work in a real streaming fashion: they wait for a complete utterance of audio before outputting a translation, or output tokens at fixed intervals, which is not suitable for real applications. This work proposes an LLM-based architecture for real streaming speech-to-text translation. The LLM learns not just to emit output tokens, but also to decide whether it has seen enough audio to do so. The system is trained using automatic alignments of the input speech and the output text. In experiments on different language pairs, the system achieves a translation quality close to the non-streaming baseline, but with a latency of only 1-2 seconds.
2605.14766.paper_summary_zh: 通常，將語音轉換為文本的系統由語音識別和文本到文本翻譯的獨立模組組成。<lb/>將這些任務結合成一個SpeechLLM有望利用語音中的副語言信息並減少級聯錯誤。<lb/>但現有的SpeechLLM系統速度較慢，因為它們並不以實時流式的方式運作：它們在輸出翻譯之前會等待完整的音頻發聲，或者以固定的間隔輸出標記，這不適合實際應用。<lb/>本研究提出了一種基於LLM的實時流式語音到文本翻譯架構。<lb/>該LLM不僅學會發出輸出標記，還能決定是否已經看到了足夠的音頻來這樣做。<lb/>系統使用輸入語音和輸出文本的自動對齊進行訓練。<lb/>在不同語言對的實驗中，該系統達到了接近非流式基準的翻譯質量，但延遲僅為1-2秒。
2605.14766.author: Titouan Parcollet et.al.
2605.14766.authors: Titouan Parcollet, Shucong Zhang, Xianrui Zheng, Rogier C. van Dalen
